key_questions: Bright red blood on toilet paper / in bowl — fresh bleeding?
Age >40 and bleeding — has colorectal cancer been excluded?
red_flags: Dark blood, melena, or blood mixed with stool → upper GI bleeding, not hemorrhoidal
Weight loss, anemia, change in bowel habits → colorectal cancer until proven otherwise
Age >50 with rectal bleeding — colonoscopy needed regardless of apparent hemorrhoidal cause
Severe acute pain + inability to sit → thrombosed external hemorrhoid (urgent care)
ddx: Anal fissure (sharp tearing pain with bowel movement)

first_line.name: Topical Hemorrhoidal Preparations
first_line.drugs: Hydrocortisone 1% + pramoxine cream (Anusol HC, Preparation H HC) TID-QID × 5–7 days | Witch hazel pads (Tucks) PRN
first_line.notes: Topical corticosteroid reduces inflammation and itch. Limit to 5–7 days (skin atrophy risk with longer use). Pramoxine: local anesthetic for pain. Apply after bowel movements and at bedtime. Witch hazel: astringent — reduces swelling and soothes.
first_line.preferred_when: Symptomatic internal or external hemorrhoids with itch and inflammation

first_line.name: Stool Softener + Fibre
first_line.drugs: Docusate sodium (Colace) 100 mg BID | Psyllium (Metamucil) 1 tbsp in water OD–BID
first_line.notes: ADDRESS THE CAUSE. Softer stool reduces straining which causes hemorrhoid formation. Psyllium fibre: most evidence for reducing hemorrhoid bleeding and symptoms. Docusate: stool softener. Adequate hydration (8 glasses/day) essential.
first_line.preferred_when: Constipation is contributing to hemorrhoids — use in all patients

second_line.name: Sitz Baths
second_line.drugs: Warm water sitz bath 15–20 min 2–3× daily
second_line.notes: Sitz baths: significant relief for external hemorrhoid discomfort. Promotes healing. Can add Epsom salts (magnesium sulfate).

non_pharm: High-fibre diet (25–35 g/day) — fruits, vegetables, whole grains
Avoid prolonged sitting on toilet (take phone elsewhere)
Avoid straining — do not hold breath while defecating (bear down gently)
Regular physical activity to promote regular bowel movements
Moist wipes instead of dry toilet paper (gentler cleansing)
treatment.refer_when: No improvement after 2 weeks of treatment; significant rectal bleeding; thrombosed external hemorrhoid (excision needed within 72h); age >40 with first episode of rectal bleeding (rule out colorectal cancer); prolapsed hemorrhoid not reducing
